stemHtml: In preparation for an RNP approach, the GNSS equipment intended to be used shows a RAIM prediction error. This means, that...

options.a: the GNSS cannot calculate the estimated time of arrival at the initial approach fix.
options.b: at the expected time of approach, the predicted navigation accuracy of the system is worse than the requirement for that approach coded in the database.
options.c: the automatic RAIM prediction function has failed and the pilot has to verify the number of available satellites with the ATC.
options.d: at the expected time of approach, an insufficient number of satellites to assure fault detection is predicted to be available.
